Scenario.title: Operation Sharpshooter: Targeting Transportation Systems
Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Simple A sophisticated spear-phishing campaign targeting transportation infrastructure leverages known vulnerabilities in industrial control systems to execute remote code and trigger denial-of-service attacks against critical operational technology.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, File, Network Traffic
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, File Modification (Mechanism), Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2018-17480 (CWE-125) : Google Chromium V8 Engine contains out-of-bounds write vulnerability that allows a remote attacker to execute code inside a sandbox via a crafted HTML page.
stage.description: The attacker deploys spear-phishing emails mimicking internal communications to trick employees into clicking malicious links hosted on internet-facing assets. These links exploit the Chromium V8 engine vulnerability to deliver payloads that steal credentials or deploy initial malware.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2019-0859 (CWE-200) : Microsoft Win32k fails to properly handle objects in memory causing privilege escalation.
stage.description: The attacker modifies legitimate system files to inject malicious code, exploiting the Win32k vulnerability to execute arbitrary code with elevated privileges on industrial control systems.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker overwhelms critical infrastructure networks with volumetric traffic floods, disrupting real-time monitoring and signaling systems by exhausting bandwidth and rendering operational technology inoperable.

Scenario.summary: A multi-stage attack begins with spear-phishing to compromise internet-facing assets, progresses to remote code execution via patched system images, and culminates in denial-of-service attacks against transportation infrastructure networks.
